perturbation.original_text: Upon the Disclosing Party’s request, the Receiving Party will promptly return to the Disclosing Party all materials or tangible items containing the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and all copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Disclosing Party’s request, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all materials or tangible items containing the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and all copies thereof,' the obligation to return copies of Confidential Information is removed. This contradicts Section 3, which mandates strict confidence and protection from disclosure, because the Receiving Party can now retain copies of confidential information, creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees: (a) to hold the other Party’s Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) to exercise at least the same care in protecting the other Party’s Confidential Information from disclosure as the Party uses with regard to its own Confidential Information; (c) not to disclose such Confidential Information to third parties; and Page 2 of 7 (d) not to use any Confidential Information for any purpose except for the Business Purpose without the prior, written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the Receiving Party to return all materials containing Confidential Information 'and all copies thereof'. The changed text removes the obligation to return 'all copies thereof'. The contradicted text states that 'Each Party agrees to hold the other Party’s Confidential Information in strict confidence' and 'not to disclose such Confidential Information to third parties'. By removing the obligation to return copies, the Receiving Party is now allowed to retain copies, which directly contradicts the requirement to hold the information in strict confidence and not disclose it. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict, fitting the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party immediately upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, or any breach of the Agreement by the Receiving Party, and will cooperate with the Disclosing Party in any reasonable fashion in order to assist the Disclosing Party regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party immediately upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, or any breach of the Agreement by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that mandates cooperation from the receiving party, creating in-text contradiction as other sections presume cooperation during breaches and unauthorised disclosures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges that all of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential information is owned solely by the Disclosing Party (and/or its licensors) and that the unauthorized disclosure or use of such Confidential Information would cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the degree of which may be difficult to ascertain and for which monetary damages would provide an insufficient remedy. Accordingly, each Party agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right to obtain an immediate injunction from any court of competent jurisdiction enjoining actual or threatened breach of this Agreement and/or actual or threatened disclosure of the Confidential Information without the necessity of posting a bond or other security. Each Party shall also have the right to pursue any other rights or remedies available at law or equity for such a breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Receiving Party to cooperate with the Disclosing Party in case of a breach. The changed text removes this cooperation requirement. The contradicted text states that unauthorized disclosure would cause irreparable harm, for which the Disclosing Party can seek an injunction. The removal of the Receiving Party's cooperation directly contradicts the Disclosing Party's implicit reliance on that cooperation to mitigate the harm mentioned in the contradicted text. Therefore, the omission creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party recognizes and agrees that nothing contained in this Agreement will be construed as granting any rights to the Receiving Party, by license or otherwise, to use any of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information except as specified in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party recognizes and agrees that nothing contained in this Agreement will be construed as granting any rights to the Receiving Party to use any of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'except as specified in this Agreement', there's an in-text contradiction on whether receiving party can use the disclosing party's Confidential Information. It creates uncertainty regarding permitted uses, contradicting other sections that might allow certain uses for the Business Purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party may disclose the other Party’s Confidential Information to its directors, officers, members, managers, employees, legal advisors, and financial advisors (each bound by obligations of confidentiality consistent with this Agreement) who have bona fide need to know, but only to the extent necessary to carry out the Business Purpose. Each Party agrees to instruct all such representatives not to disclose such Confidential Information to third parties, including consultants, without the prior written permission of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed use of confidential information 'except as specified in this Agreement'. The changed text removes this exception, creating a blanket prohibition on using confidential information. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which explicitly allows disclosure of confidential information to certain parties for the Business Purpose. The removal creates a clear and direct conflict by disallowing something that is explicitly allowed elsewhere in the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement, subject to the terms and conditions imposed on the removal of Confidential Information under paragraph 6, sets forth the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and may not be modified or amended except by written agreement executed by the parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement sets forth the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and may not be modified or amended except by written agreement executed by the parties hereto.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'subject to the terms and conditions imposed on the removal of Confidential Information under paragraph 6,' there is an in-text contradiction as the receiving party believes that it's the entire agreement between the parties, when paragraph 6 imposes separate removal conditions not covered by paragraph 14.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees not to remove any materials or tangible items containing any of the other party’s Confidential Information from the premises of the Disclosing Party without the Disclosing Party’s consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement is subject to paragraph 6, which pertains to the removal of confidential information. The changed text removes this condition, implying the agreement is the entire understanding without exception. This directly contradicts the section stating 'Each Party agrees not to remove any materials... Confidential Information... without the Disclosing Party’s consent,' creating a clear in-text contradiction by omitting the conditions of paragraph 6, rendering the removal of confidential information conditions unclear.
